channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Carroll County residents ordered to evacuate as water level rises: Here's what we know
item.description: "All we know for sure is that this is going to impact our area in a major way," Judge Executive Humphrey wrote in the evacuation order.
item.dc:creator: Corey Elam, NewsNation

item.title: The link between truancy, violent crime and how Louisiana is working to break
item.description: "Truants are often the children of parents who did not find success in their own school or value education.
item.dc:creator: Marlo Lacen, NewsNation

item.title: Tentative deal is reached in Republican fight over proxy voting for new parents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A tentative deal has been reached with the Florida Republican leading a bipartisan push to allow proxy voting in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: Dawn Staley and South Carolina hope to finish off a second straight season with an NCAA championship, which would give the Gamecocks a third title in four years. To do so, they'll need to beat Paige Bueckers and UConn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., days after President Donald Trump’s tariffs took effect.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: At least 16 people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms, flooding and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rising rivers threaten US South and Midwest after dayslong torrent of rain
item.description: Rivers rose and flooding worsened across the U.S. South and Midwest, threatening communities already waterlogged and badly damaged by days of heavy rain and wind that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner And Obed Lamy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit a sour note in landmark NYC emporium of sweets
item.description: The biggest-ticket implications of President Donald Trump's tariff blitz understandably gain the most attention.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover maker pauses shipments to US as it develops post-tariff plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The maker of Jaguar and Land Rover cars is pausing shipments to the U.S. as Britain's struggling auto industry begins to respond to the 25% tax on vehicle imports imposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increasing sleepiness linked to dementia risk in women, study finds
item.description: The research by Neurology discovered women in their eighties who experience sleep disruption have an association with cognitive aging.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KTLA 5

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Asking the Wrong Question
item.description: NewsNation Chief Washington Anchor and On Balance host Leland Vittert gives you an early look at tonight’s 9 p.m. ET show.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Invited to the Met Gala, nothing to wear? Hint: Find yourself a 'superfine' suit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — What’s in a suit? According to curators busy prepping the newest Met Gala exhibit, a whole lot more than tailoring: history, culture, identity, power and, most of all, self-expression.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Standing alongside the twisted steel tractors on his family farm in northeast Arkansas on Thursday, Danny Qualls looked on while friends and relatives helped him begin cleaning up.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: District attorney lashes out at ICE for detaining suspect in Boston during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A district attorney in Boston harshly criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Wednesday after one of its agents detained a suspect while he was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan took Wilson Martell-Lebron, 49, into custody last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Diddy civil suit thrown out after accuser refuses to identify herself
item.description: An accuser's lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs was thrown out this week after "Jane Doe" refused to disclose her identity.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, WGN 9

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: It is the first time the Justice Department has sought to bring the death penalty since President Donald Trump returned to office in January.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WGN 9
